## Freelance Service Agreement

### Preamble

This Freelance Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into and made effective as of January 15, 2025 ("Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., a California corporation, on behalf of Client represented by Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, operated by Alex Chen, Senior Designer ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer are each a "Party" and together the "Parties."

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to retain Freelancer to perform design and branding services in connection with a project titled "Brand Refresh 2025"; and WHEREAS, Freelancer has the professional expertise and agrees to perform the services described herein under the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises contained herein, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## Clauses (23 total)

### Scope of Services

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will perform professional design services for Client for the project "Brand Refresh 2025," consisting of a complete visual identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document. Deliverables, due dates, and allocation of fees are: (a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due January 30, 2025 — $2,000; (b) Selected logo refinement — due February 15, 2025 — $1,500; (c) Brand guidelines document — due March 1, 2025 — $2,500; (d) Asset package delivery (final files for print and web, color specs, fonts/links, export formats) — due March 15, 2025 — $2,500. Freelancer shall provide up to three (3) rounds of revisions as set forth in Clause 6. Any work outside this scope constitutes a change order and will be billed in accordance with Clause 7.

### Term and Project Deadline

*Type: standard*

The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and will continue until completion of the Services and delivery of all Deliverables, unless earlier terminated under Clause 12. The overall project deadline for all Deliverables is March 15, 2025. Time is of the essence with respect to the deadlines set forth in this Agreement. Delivery dates may be adjusted only by written agreement of the Parties or as set forth in Clause 8 (Force Majeure).

### Compensation and Payment Terms

*Type: standard*

Total fee for the Services is Eight Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($8,500) ("Total Amount"). Client shall pay a non-refundable deposit of Two Thousand Five Hundred Fifty Dollars ($2,550) upon execution of this Agreement. The deposit shall be credited against invoices in the following manner: the deposit will first be applied to the first milestone(s) invoiced; any excess deposit balance will be applied to subsequent invoices. Freelancer will invoice the Client upon delivery of each milestone. Milestone amounts are allocated as follows: Logo concepts (3 options) — $2,000; Selected logo refinement — $1,500; Brand guidelines document — $2,500; Asset package delivery — $2,500. Payment for each invoice is due within seven (7) calendar days of receipt of invoice. Any amounts not paid when due shall accrue interest at the rate of 1.5% per month (or the maximum rate permitted by law, if less) from the due date until paid. If Client fails to pay any undisputed invoice within ten (10) days following written notice, Freelancer may suspend work until payment is received.

### Deposit and Expenses

*Type: standard*

The deposit is non-refundable except as expressly provided herein. Client agrees to reimburse Freelancer for pre-approved out-of-pocket expenses reasonably incurred in connection with the Services (e.g., stock imagery, typeface licensing, third-party vendor fees). Such expenses will be itemized on invoices. Freelancer will obtain Client's prior written approval for any single expense in excess of Two Hundred Dollars ($200).

### Revision Rounds

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will include up to three (3) rounds of revisions per Deliverable. A "round" means a single set of changes submitted by Client in response to a Deliverable. Revisions beyond the included rounds, or requests that materially change previously approved concepts, will be considered additional work and billed at Freelancer's standard hourly rate of One Hundred Dollars ($100) per hour, or at a mutually agreed fixed fee. Additional revision work will be commenced only after Client's written approval of the associated cost.

### Change Orders

*Type: standard*

Any request by Client to modify the scope, schedule, or deliverables shall be submitted in writing and will be effective only if accepted by Freelancer in writing as a Change Order, which may include adjustments to fees, schedules, and deliverables. Freelancer is not required to perform services beyond the Scope of Services without an executed Change Order.

### Client Responsibilities and Approvals

*Type: standard*

Client shall provide timely access to information, materials, approvals, and feedback reasonably required by Freelancer to perform the Services. Client’s failure to respond or approve in a timely manner may result in schedule delays and additional fees. Client represents that any materials it provides do not infringe third-party rights. Client shall be responsible for obtaining any necessary permissions and licenses for materials provided to Freelancer.

### Delivery; Acceptance

*Type: standard*

Freelancer will deliver the Deliverables in electronic format to Client at the agreed milestones. Client will review delivered work and provide any requested feedback or approval within five (5) business days. Failure to respond within five (5) business days shall be deemed acceptance. Upon Client acceptance and full payment of all fees due for a Deliverable (including any outstanding invoices), ownership and rights shall transfer as set forth in Clause 10.

### Ownership, Copyright, and License

*Type: standard*

Subject to Client's full payment of all fees and expenses due under this Agreement, Freelancer assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, including copyrights ("Assigned IP"). Freelancer retains ownership of and all rights in Freelancer's pre-existing materials, tools, templates, methodologies, and any third-party materials not owned by Freelancer (collectively, "Retained Materials"). To the extent any Retained Materials are included in the Deliverables, Freelancer grants Client a non-exclusive, worldwide, perpetual license to use such Retained Materials as incorporated into the Deliverables for Client's business purposes. Freelancer reserves the right to include Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, marketing materials, and website unless Client provides written objection prior to execution. If moral rights exist under applicable law, Freelancer hereby waives and agrees not to assert such rights to the maximum extent permitted by law.

### Warranties and Representations

*Type: standard*

Each Party represents and warrants that it has the power and authority to enter into this Agreement. Freelancer represents that the Deliverables will be original works prepared by Freelancer and will not knowingly infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party. Except as expressly provided, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

Client shall indemnify, defend and hold Freelancer harmless from and against any losses, damages, liabilities, claims, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of Client's use of the Deliverables, Client's breach of this Agreement, or Client-provided materials that infringe third-party rights. Freelancer shall indemnify, defend and hold Client harmless from and against any losses, damages, liabilities, claims, costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees) arising from a claim that the Deliverables, as delivered by Freelancer and used as authorized herein, infringe a third party's copyright, provided Client gives Freelancer prompt written notice of such claim and cooperates in the defense.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

EXCEPT FOR A PARTY'S INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS OR WILLFUL MISCONDUCT, IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR CONSEQUENTIAL, INCIDENTAL, EXEMPLARY, PUNITIVE OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING LOST PROFITS, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE. FREELANCER'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY FOR ANY CLAIM ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT SHALL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL FEES PAID BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

Each Party shall hold in confidence and not disclose the other Party's confidential information, including pricing, business practices, product development, trade secrets, and any non-public information designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential. Confidential information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly known through no breach by the receiving Party; (b) is rightfully received from a third party without restriction; (c) is independently developed by the receiving Party; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law or court order (provided prompt notice to the disclosing Party). The obligations of confidentiality survive termination of this Agreement for three (3) years.

### Termination

*Type: standard*

Either Party may terminate this Agreement for material breach by the other Party if the breaching Party fails to cure the breach within ten (10) calendar days after written notice. Client may terminate for convenience upon fourteen (14) calendar days' prior written notice. Upon termination, Client shall pay Freelancer for all Services performed and expenses incurred up to the effective date of termination, including any non-cancellable commitments, and Freelancer shall deliver to Client all work-in-progress. The deposit is non-refundable except to the extent Freelancer returns unearned fees as calculated by pro rata allocation of the Total Amount less work already performed. Sections regarding Payment, Ownership, Indemnification, Limitation of Liability, Confidentiality, Governing Law, Dispute Resolution, and Survival shall survive termination.

### Force Majeure

*Type: standard*

Neither Party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform due to causes beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, pandemics, labor disputes, government action, or failure of suppliers, provided the affected Party gives prompt notice and uses reasonable efforts to mitigate the delay. If a force majeure event continues for more than thirty (30) days, either Party may terminate this Agreement with written notice and Client shall pay for Services performed through the termination date.

### Dispute Resolution; Governing Law and Venue

*Type: standard*

The Parties shall first attempt to resolve any dispute arising under or related to this Agreement through good-faith negotiation. If the Parties cannot resolve the dispute within thirty (30) days, they agree to attempt mediation in California. If mediation fails, any unresolved controversy or claim shall be resolved by binding arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association (or another mutually agreed arbitration provider) conducted in San Francisco County, California. Judgment upon the award may be entered in any court of competent jurisdiction. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to its conflicts of law principles.

### Independent Contractor; Taxes

*Type: standard*

Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, or partner of Client. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, benefits, and insurance related to amounts paid under this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create an employment relationship.

### Insurance

*Type: optional*

During the term of this Agreement, Freelancer shall maintain commercially reasonable insurance coverage appropriate for the Services, including general liability insurance. Upon Client's request, Freelancer will provide proof of insurance to Client.

### Publicity

*Type: optional*

Freelancer may identify Client as a client and may display the Deliverables in Freelancer's portfolio, website, and marketing materials unless Client provides written objection at the time of signing. If Client objects, Parties will negotiate a reasonable alternative.

### Non-Solicitation

*Type: optional*

During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months thereafter, Client agrees not to directly solicit or hire Freelancer personnel who substantially participated in performance of the Services without Freelancer's prior written consent. This provision shall not apply to general solicitations not specifically directed at Freelancer employees or contractors.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by certified mail (return receipt requested), or by nationally recognized overnight courier to the addresses set forth below or such other addresses as may be designated by written notice: Client: Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director, TechStartup Inc.; Freelancer: Alex Chen, Chen Creative Studio. Electronic notices (email) shall be effective when receipt is confirmed by the receiving Party.

### Severability; Amendment; Entire Agreement

*Type: standard*

If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect. This Agreement may be amended only by a writing signed by both Parties. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, whether written or oral.

### Counterparts; Electronic Signatures

*Type: standard*

This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which together shall constitute one instrument. Signatures transmitted by electronic means (e.g., PDF or facsimile) shall be deemed original signatures for all purposes.
